questions.question: What does HTML stand for?
questions.explanation: HTML stands for HyperText Markup Language and is used to structure web pages.

questions.question: Which language is used for styling web pages?
questions.explanation: CSS (Cascading Style Sheets) is used to style HTML elements.

questions.question: Which language runs in the browser?
questions.explanation: JavaScript runs directly in the browser and powers interactive websites.

questions.question: What does CSS stand for?
questions.explanation: CSS stands for Cascading Style Sheets and controls the appearance of web pages.

questions.question: What does JSON stand for?
questions.explanation: JSON stands for JavaScript Object Notation and is used to exchange data.

questions.question: Which HTTP method is commonly used to retrieve data?
questions.explanation: GET is used to retrieve data from a server.

questions.question: Which company created JavaScript?
questions.explanation: JavaScript was originally created at Netscape by Brendan Eich.

questions.question: Which tool is commonly used for version control?
questions.explanation: Git is the most widely used version control system.

questions.question: What command uploads local commits to a remote Git repository?
questions.explanation: git push sends your local commits to the remote repository.

questions.question: Which HTML tag is used to create a hyperlink?
questions.explanation: The <a> (anchor) tag is used to create hyperlinks in HTML.

questions.question: Which symbol is used to select an id in CSS?
questions.explanation: The # symbol selects elements by their id in CSS.

questions.question: Which JavaScript function is used to display text in the console?
questions.explanation: console.log() prints messages to the browser console for debugging.
